quick_skim.title: SemiScope: Disentangling Classifier Tuning and Joint Optimization in Semi-Supervised Security Classification
quick_skim.abstract: Background. Labeled data for security classification is scarce. Semi-supervised learning (SSL) propagates labels from a small labeled pool to larger unlabeled pools. Yet security applications often use SSL as a black box: default parameters, a fixed classifier, and no handling of pseudo-label-induced class imbalance. Aims. Recent work reports sizeable gains from optimizing SSL pipelines via joint search, AutoML, or per-component tuning. These gains are hard to attribute: they may reflect useful SSL-classifier interactions, or mostly from simply tuning the downstream classifier. We disentangle these effects for binary tabular security data with classical SSL and tree-based classifiers. Method. We build SemiScope as an analysis instrument, not a deployment recommendation. It uses Bayesian Optimization to jointly tune SSL settings, confidence filtering, oversampling, and the classifier. The key control, Tuned-Clf, fixes SSL to defaults but gets the same 100-trial classifier budget and validation-set threshold tuning as SemiScope. At 10% labels, we compare them with paired TOST using a +/-1.0 g-measure smallest effect of interest. Results. SemiScope beats every default SSL baseline on all five datasets, improving over the strongest by 0.7-12.7 points. Under the equal-budget control, Tuned-Clf is statistically equivalent to the full pipeline on 4 of 5 datasets; Phishing is inconclusive. Classifier HPO alone recovers a median 86% of SemiScope's gain over Default Self-Training (ST) + Random Forest (RF). Conclusions. The reusable contribution is the decomposition protocol. A simpler recipe suffices: use Self-Training, tune the classifier with Bayesian Optimization, and tune the decision threshold on validation data. It reaches within 1 g-measure of Supervised RF at 20-30% labels on four datasets and 40% on Drebin, at the same or lower label rate than Default ST + RF on every dataset.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: semi-supervised learning for security classification applicable to encrypted traffic
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 半监督学习方法可应用于加密流量分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 半监督学习方法可应用于加密流量分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 半监督学习方法可应用于加密流量分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SemiScope to disentangle SSL classifier tuning effects; method transferable to encrypted traffic classification with scarce labels.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 半监督学习在安全分类中常被当作黑箱使用，参数和分类器固定，未处理伪标签引起的不平衡。SemiScope框架通过系统比较单独调优分类器与联合搜索整个SSL流水线，解耦了两种优化的贡献。在表格安全数据上的实验表明，大部分性能提升来自下游分类器调优，简化了SSL在加密流量分类等标签稀缺情况下的应用。该工作为半监督安全分类提供了可复现的基准和实用建议。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 半监督学习在安全分类中常被当作黑箱使用，参数和分类器固定，未处理伪标签引起的不平衡。SemiScope框架通过系统比较单独调优分类器与联合搜索整个SSL流水线，解耦了两种优化的贡献。在表格安全数据上的实验表明，大部分性能提升来自下游分类器调优，简化了SSL在加密流量分类等标签稀缺情况下的应用。该工作为半监督安全分类提供了可复现的基准和实用建议。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: machine learning techniques for encrypted traffic
